sections.paragraph:
ἐλλόγιμος, ον (s. prec. entry; Hdt. et al.) prim. ‘pert to someth. being taken into account or special consideration’, hence

① included ἐντεταγμένος καὶ ἐ. εἰς τ. ἀριθμὸν τῶν σῳζομένων enrolled and included in the number of those who are saved 1 Cl 58:2. μικροὶ καὶ ἐλλόγιμοι small, but included 57:2.

② reputable, eminent (SIG 803, 8; OGI 56, 9; Philo, Mos. 1, 266) ἄνδρες ἐ. (Philostrat., Vi. Apoll. p. 50, 31; Herm. Wr. 12, 6; Celsus 1, 21) 1 Cl 44:3. Superl. ἐλλογιμώτατος highly regarded ἄνδρες (in sg. Philostrat., Vi. Apoll. p. 106, 32; 112, 26; honorific in Byz. pap) 62:3.—DELG s.v. λέγω B2. Sv.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

prim. prim. = primarily

someth. someth. = something

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Herm. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Celsus Celsus , in Origen, Contra Celsum, II a.d.—List 5

  1. sg. = singular

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 319
